headline: E-Commerce Fraud Reports Surge 45% to 85,000 Cases
context: KPDNHEP received 85,000 e-commerce fraud reports in 2025 with non-delivery and counterfeits as top complaints.

cards.big: E-commerce fraud reports surged 45% to 85,000 in 2025
cards.sub: Non-delivery counterfeit goods and misleading ads dominate complaints

cards.big: Consumer losses reached RM480 million with recovery below 15%
cards.sub: Average loss per complaint RM5,600 with slow resolution

cards.big: Cross-border purchases at 42% of complaints beyond Malaysian jurisdiction
cards.sub: Chinese platform sellers subject of 28,000 complaints for counterfeits

cards.big: KPDNHEP acted on only 4,200 of 85,000 complaints due to capacity
cards.sub: 280 enforcement officers for all consumer protection nationwide

cards.big: E-commerce fraud tests whether consumer protection can scale to match online transaction volume
85,000 e-commerce fraud cases — 45% surge. Consumer trust erodes faster than platforms can rebuild it.
